questions.q: A set of lines $x+y-2+\lambda_1(2 x+y-3)=0$ represents incident rays on an ellipse $S=0$ and $2 x+3 y-23+\lambda_2(2 x-y-3)=0$ represents the set of reflective rays from the ellipse where $\lambda_1, \lambda_2 \in \mathrm{R}$. If $\mathrm{P}(3,7)$ is a point on the ellipse normal at which meets the major axis at N .
options: N divides line segment joining two foci in the ratio $2 \sqrt{2}: 1$
Area of triangle formed by point $P$ and two foci 5
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the normal at any given point $P$ on ellipse $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ meets its auxiliary circle at $Q$ and $R$ such that $\angle \mathrm{QOR}=90^{\circ}$, where O is centre of ellipse, then
questions.solution:
Homogenizing auxiliary circle with normal at $P$
$x^2+y^2=\frac{a^2(a x \sec \theta-b y \operatorname{cosec} \theta)^2}{\left(a^2-b^2\right)^2}$
$\angle Q O R=90^{\circ} \Rightarrow-1+\frac{a^4 \sec ^2 \theta}{\left(a^2-b^2\right)^2}-1+\frac{a^2 b^2 \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta}{\left(a^2-b^2\right)^2}=0$
$2\left(a^2-b^2\right)^2=a^4 \sec ^2 \theta+a^2 b^2 \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta$
$a^4-5 a^2 b^2+2 b^4=a^4 \tan ^2 \theta+a^2 b^2 \cot ^2 \theta \geq 2 a^3 b$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ellipse is orthogonal to the hyperbola $x^2-y^2=2$. The eccentricity of the ellipse is reciprocal of that of the hyperbola. Then:
options: equation of the ellipse is $x^2+2 y^2=8$
focus of the ellipse is at $(-4 \sqrt{2}, 0)$
equation of directrix of ellipse is $x+4 \sqrt{2}=0$
equation of director circle of ellipse is $x^2+y^2=12$
questions.solution:
Eccentricity of ellipse $=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
Let equation of ellipse be $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{a^2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)}=1$
$$
x^2-y^2=2
$$
As eqns. (1) and (2) intersect orthogonally, so $\left.\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right]_{(1)} \times \frac{d y}{d x}\right]_{(2)}=-1$ at point of intersection.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider a conic $C: y^2=4 x$. Let $\triangle P Q R$ be an equilateral triangle with side length $k$ where $P$ be any point on $C, Q$ be the foot of perpendicular from $P$ upon the directrix of $C$ and $R$ be the focus of $C$. A circle $C_2$ is inscribed in another conic $C_1: y^2=k(x+1)$ which touches $C_1$ at the points where $C_1$ cuts the $y$-axis. $C_3$ is an ellipse whose auxiliary circle is $C_2$ and major axis coincides with the axis of symmetry of $C_1$ and whose length of minor axis is 4.Question: Identify the correct statement(s) for $\mathrm{C}_3$.
questions.solution:
We have $P M=1+t^2$ $\begin{aligned} & P S=\sqrt{\left(t^2-1\right)^2+4 t^2}=\left(t^2+1\right) \\ & M S=\sqrt{4+4 t^2}=2 \sqrt{1+t^2} \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{1+t^2}=1+t^2 \end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad P M=1+t^2=4=a=k \text { (Given) }$ Hence $C_1: y^2=4(x+1)$ Equation of tangent to $C_1$ at $(0,2)$ is $\begin{aligned} & 2 y=4\left(\frac{x+0}{2}+1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow y=x+2 \end{aligned}$ Now circle which touches above line at $(0,2)$, is $x^2+(y-2)^2+\lambda(x-y+2)=0$. As above circle is passing through the point $(0,-2)$, so
$\begin{aligned} & 0+16+\lambda(4)=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=-4 \\ & \therefore \quad C_2: x^2+(y-2)^2-4(x-y+2)=0 \\ & \text { or, } C_2: x^2+y^2-4 x-4=0\end{aligned}$ Now $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=1, \quad \mathrm{a}=2 \sqrt{2}$ and $\mathrm{b}=2$ So $\mathrm{C}_3: \frac{(\mathrm{x}-2)^2}{8}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{4}=1$. (i) Given $\mathrm{C}_1: \mathrm{y}^2=4(\mathrm{x}+1)$ (A) Minimum length of focal chord $=$ Latus rectum $=4$. (B) Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents $=$ Director circle which is $\mathrm{x}+2=0$. (C) Clearly distance between focus and tangent at vertex is 1. (D) Foot of the directrix is clearly $(-2,0)$. (ii) We have $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{8}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ (A) $e=\sqrt{1-\frac{4}{8}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ (B) Focal length $=2$ ae $=2 \times 2 \sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=4$ (C) Latus-rectum $=\frac{2 b^2}{a}=2\left(\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)=2 \sqrt{2}$ (D) Director circle is $(x-2)^2+y^2=12 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-8=0$
(iii) Triangle formed by common tangents to the curves $\mathrm{C}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_2$ and latus-rectum of $\mathrm{C}_1$, is isosceles triangle. Required area $=\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 2=4$ square units.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the ellipse $\frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{\tan ^2 \alpha}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\sec ^2 \alpha}=1$ where $\alpha \in(0, \pi / 2)$.
Which of the following quantities would vary as $\alpha$ varies?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{e}^2=1-\frac{\tan ^2 \alpha}{\sec ^2 \alpha}=\cos ^2 \alpha \quad\left(\text { as } \sec ^2 \alpha\gt\tan ^2 \alpha\right) \\ & \text { hence } e=\cos \alpha ; \operatorname{vertex}(0, \pm \sec \alpha) \\ & \text { foci }=(0,1) ; l(\mathrm{LR})=\frac{2 b^2}{a}=\frac{2 \tan ^2 \alpha}{\sec \alpha}=2 \sin \alpha \cdot \tan \alpha\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A straight line through a point $P(\alpha, 2),(\alpha \neq 0)$ meets the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{9}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ at $A$ and $D \&$ meets axes at $B$ and $C$ such that PA, PB, PC and PD are in GP, then ' $\alpha$ ' can lie in interval
questions.solution:
Any point on the line through $P(\alpha, 2)$ is $(\alpha+r \cos \theta, 2+r \sin \theta)$ and meets the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{9}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ at
A and D
$\Rightarrow \frac{(\alpha+r \cos \theta)^2}{9}+\frac{(2+r \sin \theta)^2}{4}=1$
$\Rightarrow\left(4 \cos ^2 \theta+9 \sin ^2 \theta\right) r^2+4(2 \alpha \cos \theta+9 \sin \theta) r+4 \alpha^2=0$
$\Rightarrow P A \cdot P D=\frac{4 \alpha^2}{4 \cos ^2 \theta+9 \sin ^2 \theta}$
Again solving with $x y=0$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{PB} \cdot \mathrm{PC}=\frac{2 \alpha}{\sin \theta \cos \theta}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4 \alpha^2}{4 \cos ^2 \theta+9 \sin ^2 \theta}=\frac{2 \alpha}{\sin \theta \cos \theta}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 2 \alpha \sin 2 \theta+5 \cos 2 \theta=13 \\ & \Rightarrow-1 \leq \frac{13}{\sqrt{4 \alpha^2+25}} \leq 1 \Rightarrow|\alpha| \geq 6\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a number of ellipse be described having the same major axis $2 a$ but a variable minor axis then the tangents at the ends of their latera recta pass through fixed points which can be
questions.solution:
$e$ is a variable quantity
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x a e}{a^2}+\frac{y b^2}{a b^2}=1 \\
& \Rightarrow e x+y=a \\
& \Rightarrow y-a+e x=0
\end{aligned}$
it passes through $(0, a)$.
Similarly, other point is $(0,-\mathrm{a})$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The ends of the major axis of ellipse are $(-2,4)$ and $(2,1)$. If the point $(1,3)$ lies on the ellipse. Then:
options: The length of major axis is equal to 10 .
The length of minor axis is equal to $\frac{10}{\sqrt{24}}$.
The length of latus rectum of ellipse is $\frac{5}{6}$.
Square of the distance between the focii of ellipse is $\frac{125}{6}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 2 a=\sqrt{16+9}=5 \\ & C \equiv\left(0, \frac{5}{2}\right) \\ & F_1 \equiv\left[2 e, \frac{5}{2}(5-3 e)\right] \\ & F_2 \equiv\left[-2 e, \frac{1}{2}(5+3 e)\right]\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P F_1+P F_2=2 a \\ & e^2=\frac{5}{6} \quad \Rightarrow \quad e=\sqrt{\frac{5}{6}} \\ & \end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad b^2=\frac{25}{4}$, now verify all the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $(5,12)$ and $(24,7)$ are the foci of a conic passing through the origin then the eccentricity of conic is
questions.solution:
Distance between foci $=\sqrt{19^2+5^2}=\sqrt{386}$
Now by PS + S'P = 2a (for ellipse)
(take point $P$ at origin) we get $a=19$
$\therefore \quad 2 a e=\sqrt{386} \Rightarrow e=\frac{\sqrt{386}}{38}$
If conic is hyperbola
$\left|P S-P S^{\prime}\right|=2 a \Rightarrow a=6$
by 2ae' $=\sqrt{386}$
$\mathrm{e}^{\prime}=\frac{\sqrt{386}}{12}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ellipse with eccentricity $\frac{1}{2}$ passes through $P(3,4)$ whose nearer focus is $S(0,0)$ and equation of tangent at $P$ on ellipse is $3 x+4 y-25=0$. If a chord through $S$ parallel to tangent at $P$ intersects the ellipse at $A$ and $B$ then:
options: length of latus rectum of ellipse is 15
questions.solution:
$\because \quad P(3,4)$ is foot of perpendicular from $S(0,0)$ 
on $3 x+4 y-25=0$ which is also on ellipse
$\therefore \quad P$ is vertex of ellipse.
$\therefore \quad$ Distance between focus and directrix $=a-a e=5$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad a-\frac{a}{2}=5 \Rightarrow \quad a=10 \\ & \because \quad b^2=a^2\left(1-e^2\right)=100\left(1-\frac{1}{4}\right)=75\end{aligned}$
Clearly $A B$ will be latus rectum of ellipse.
$\therefore \quad A B=$ length of $L R=\frac{2 b^2}{a}=15$
$\therefore \quad$ Focal length $=2 a e=10$
Mid-point of vertex and centre will focus.
$\therefore \quad \text { centre }=(-3,-4)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
